title: For a Hard Diagnosis
scripture_text: Your eyes saw my unformed substance; in your book were written, every one of them, the days that were formed for me, when as yet there was none of them.
reflection: Your body is not a thing that betrayed you. It is a body that is going through something. There is a difference.
body:
The word the doctor used is going to live in your head for a while. Maybe forever. It is a strange word, a medical word, and it does not have the feel of a word that belongs to your life. But it does, now.

You are allowed to be afraid. You are allowed to be angry. You are allowed to sit on the bathroom floor and not be okay.

The Lord is not the author of the disease. He is the one sitting beside you in the waiting room. He is the one who, in some way past understanding, has numbered your days, and is in all of them. None of them are wasted. None of them are outside His care.

You are not alone. You are not your diagnosis. You are still you, beloved, seen, and held.
prayer: Lord, walk with me through this. I do not understand it. I do not have to. Be my strength. Amen.
